title: podman-commands script: refactor
body:
Make more general-purpose: instead of hardcoding a list
of known subcommands, and duplicating sed pipelines for
each, rely on 'podman help' itself to tell us which
podman commands have subcommands; and examine each
in turn. Should there ever be new subcommands, this
will identify and test them.

A special case is needed for 'podman image trust', whose
documentation format doesn't match the others.

The change to `common.go` fixes an inconsistency: the
Usage message for commands with subcommands had an
unnecessary blank line, making it harder to parse
automatically. This simply produces consistent
Usage messages for all podman commands.

This script will not pass until #2480 is merged.
After that, the goal is to add this as a CI hook.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

comments.body:
This is only testing podman, not the subcommands?

podman-volume, podman-pod, podman-container, podman-image, podman-generate, podman-play?
Seems to work and I'm not seeing anything code wise.  I'll give it a LGTM but would like nods from at least @mheon and @rhatdan

I don't see this working.  I am seeing less problems then when I run my script.

I know there is differences between podman image and podman container but the only output I see is:

Maybe because master changed? I've just rebased against new master, including #2480, and force-pushed. I now see: no differences in podman; `podman container` documenting `ls`, `ps`, and `unmount` in man but not in help; and `podman image` documenting ls in man but not help.

@rhatdan I found one difference between your script and mine, and it's an actual bug in `docs/podman-image.1.md`: the `rm` line correctly links to podman-rmi.1.md, but the link text (in brackets) is `podman-rm`.
That's such a small change it barely deserves a PR; should I address it in here?

Sigh...... okay, that's not the only such instance:
```
for i in container image system play  volume pod;do echo $i;perl -ne 'if (/^\|.*\[(podman-.*?)\(1\)\]\((podman-.*?)\.1\.md/) { print "  $1 != $2\n" if $1 ne $2 }' <docs/podman-$i.1.md;done
container
  podman-exists != podman-container-exists
  podman-refresh != podman-container-refresh
image
  podman-exists != podman-image-exists
  podman-rm != podman-rmi
system
  podman-system-info != podman-info
play
volume
pod
```
Time for another CI check?

ginkgo failed, but it doesn't look related to anything I did

/retest
